message:
test(lab): add FrankenLab integration test suite (bd-1hu19.5)

32 integration tests covering scenario YAML loading, execution of all
10 example scenarios, proptest roundtrip, deterministic replay across
100 runs, fault injection determinism, validation error handling, seed
exploration, and JSON output format. Also fixes clock_skew_lease.yaml
which referenced a non-existent 'determinism' oracle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

 {"id":"bd-1hu19.2","title":"[WEDGE] Implement deterministic scheduler for replay","description":"Implement a deterministic scheduler for FrankenLab that provides exactly reproducible execution of async tasks. The scheduler must: (1) Use a seeded PRNG for all nondeterministic scheduling decisions, (2) Record all scheduling choices to a trace file (franken_trace format), (3) Replay from a trace file with identical decisions, (4) Support exploration strategies: random, round-robin, DPOR, PCT. Replaces tokio runtime for testing via #[frankenlab::test(seed=42)]. Performance: <10% overhead for small tests, up to 5x for large tests with full recording. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: scheduler::step with step_index, strategy, seed, runnable_count. Span: scheduler::record with trace_file, total_steps. Span: scheduler::replay with trace_file, expected_steps. DEBUG: scheduling decision: step={idx}, chose_task={task_id}, runnable=[{task_ids}], strategy={strategy}. DEBUG: trace event recorded: step={idx}, event_type={type}, task_id={task_id}. INFO: scheduler initialized: seed={seed}, strategy={strategy}, task_count={n}. INFO: recording started: trace_file={path}. INFO: replay started: trace_file={path}, expected_steps={n}. INFO: execution completed: total_steps={n}, wall_time_ms={ms}, virtual_time_ms={vms}. WARN: replay divergence detected: step={idx}, expected_task={expected}, actual_runnable=[{tasks}]. ERROR: replay failed: step={idx}, reason={reason}. Metrics: scheduler_step_total (counter by strategy), scheduler_step_latency_us (histogram), scheduler_runnable_count (histogram), scheduler_replay_divergence_total (counter), trace_file_size_bytes (gauge).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:20:52.710624316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:00:40.813021931Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:00:40.812886759Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deterministic","frankenlab","scheduler","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hu19.3","title":"[WEDGE] Implement virtual time for testing","description":"Implement virtual time system for FrankenLab enabling programmatic time control. Features: (1) Instant timeout testing -- advance time by 30s without waiting, (2) Deterministic timing -- all time-dependent ops use virtual clock, (3) Clock manipulation -- pause, resume, set, advance by delta. Implementation: (a) VirtualClock struct with atomic current_time, (b) Hook into tokio::time::sleep for virtual clock, (c) Auto-advance when all tasks blocked on sleep, (d) Manual advance via clock.advance(Duration), (e) Integration with fault injection for clock skew. Must handle ordered wake-ups, timer cancellation, I/O timeouts. Acceptance: 24-hour wall-clock test completes in <1 second with virtual time. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: virtual_clock::advance with delta_ms, new_time_ms, wakeups_triggered. DEBUG: virtual clock advanced: delta_ms={delta}, new_time_ms={new_time}, wakeup_count={n}. DEBUG: virtual clock auto-advance: reason=all_tasks_blocked, next_wakeup_ms={next}. INFO: virtual clock initialized: start_time_ms={start}. INFO: virtual clock paused/resumed at time_ms={time}. WARN: virtual clock jump detected: old_time_ms={old}, new_time_ms={new}, jump_ms={jump} -- may affect lease/timeout correctness. ERROR: virtual clock attempt to go backward: current_ms={current}, requested_ms={requested}. Metrics: virtual_clock_advances_total (counter), virtual_clock_auto_advances_total (counter), virtual_clock_wakeups_triggered_total (counter), virtual_clock_current_ms (gauge), virtual_clock_wall_to_virtual_ratio (gauge).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:01.702297094Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:35:59.378249568Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:35:06.739752299Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","virtual-time","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:53Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:53Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1414,"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"MagentaElk: Verified bd-1hu19.3 complete. Virtual time infrastructure already implemented. Added missing logging entries from spec (INFO init, DEBUG advance, ERROR backward). All 27 tests pass. 24h acceptance test in 0.1s. Committed as 49026d4.","created_at":"2026-02-14T01:35:59Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hu19.4","title":"[WEDGE] FrankenLab CLI: frankenlab run scenario.yaml","description":"Build the FrankenLab CLI tool that runs test scenarios from YAML files. Command: frankenlab run scenario.yaml. Behavior: (1) Parse and validate scenario YAML against JSON Schema, (2) Set up participants with initial state, (3) Initialize deterministic scheduler with seed, (4) Execute scenario with fault injection per schedule, (5) Check assertions, (6) Output results as JSON or human-readable. Additional commands: validate, replay, minimize, compare. Exit codes: 0=pass, 1=assertion failure, 2=scenario error. Must produce clear, actionable error messages. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: cli::run with scenario_file, seed, command. Span: cli::validate with scenario_file. Span: cli::replay with trace_file. Span: cli::minimize with scenario_file, strategy. DEBUG: CLI argument parsing: command={cmd}, args={args_json}. INFO: frankenlab {command} started: scenario={file}, seed={seed}. INFO: frankenlab {command} completed: exit_code={code}, duration_ms={ms}, assertions_passed={p}, assertions_failed={f}. WARN: scenario completed with warnings: warning_count={n}, warnings=[{warning_msgs}]. ERROR: scenario execution failed: error={err}, at_step={step}, virtual_time_ms={vt}. ERROR: CLI usage error: {message} (must include suggestion for fix). Metrics: cli_execution_total (counter by command and exit_code), cli_execution_duration_ms (histogram by command), cli_assertions_total (counter by result=pass|fail).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:09.781021657Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:43:29.231422185Z","closed_at":"2026-02-14T01:43:29.231276404Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","frankenlab","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1hu19.5","title":"[WEDGE] Unit and E2E tests for FrankenLab runtime and CLI","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the FrankenLab standalone testing harness, covering the deterministic scheduler, virtual time, scenario YAML format, and CLI.

**Unit tests:**
(1) Deterministic scheduler reproducibility — run the same program with the same seed 100 times; verify bit-identical scheduling decisions each time.
(2) PRNG scheduling strategies — verify that random, round-robin, DPOR, and PCT strategies each produce valid (non-deadlocking) schedules for a standard test program.
(3) Virtual time correctness — verify that sleep(Duration::from_secs(N)) with virtual time completes instantly in wall-clock time but advances virtual clock by exactly N seconds.
(4) Auto-advance semantics — when all tasks block on sleep, virtual time advances to the minimum next wake-up point (not further).
(5) YAML scenario parser — roundtrip property: parse(serialize(scenario)) == scenario for randomly generated scenarios (proptest).
(6) JSON Schema validation — verify that all example scenarios pass schema validation and that intentionally invalid scenarios are rejected with clear errors.
(7) Fault injection determinism — the same fault schedule with the same seed produces identical fault timing across runs.

**E2E tests:**
(8) Full scenario execution — run all 10+ example scenarios through the CLI and verify all assertions pass.
(9) Record/replay round-trip — record an execution trace, replay it, and verify bit-identical outcomes.
(10) Cross-machine reproducibility — generate artifact hashes on CI and verify they match expected golden values (proves determinism is not hardware-dependent).
(11) CLI error handling — verify that malformed YAML, missing files, and invalid seeds produce clear, actionable error messages with non-zero exit codes.
(12) Performance regression — FrankenLab overhead vs direct execution must stay below 10% for small tests (criterion.rs benchmark).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test must log: scenario_name, seed, strategy, virtual_time_final, wall_time_ms, assertion_results, fault_count.
- On replay divergence, log the first diverging scheduling decision with full context.
- All logs in structured JSON to EvidenceLedger.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- 100% deterministic replay across 1000 runs.
- All example scenarios pass.
- Overhead below 10% for small tests, below 5x for large tests.
- All CLI error cases produce exit code != 0 with human-readable message.

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for YAML schema fuzzing.
- Use assert_cmd + predicates for CLI integration tests.
- Use tempfile for isolated test directories.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:32.345542617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:38.411148668Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","testing","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}

**Unit tests:**

(11) CLI error handling — verify that malformed YAML, missing files, and invalid seeds produce clear, actionable error messages with non-zero exit codes.
(12) Performance regression — FrankenLab overhead vs direct execution must stay below 10% for small tests (criterion.rs benchmark).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test must log: scenario_name, seed, strategy, virtual_time_final, wall_time_ms, assertion_results, fault_count.
- On replay divergence, log the first diverging scheduling decision with full context.
- All logs in structured JSON to EvidenceLedger.

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for YAML schema fuzzing.
- Use assert_cmd + predicates for CLI integration tests.
- Use tempfile for isolated test directories.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaElk","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:32.345542617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T01:52:44.323138038Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlab","testing","wedge"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hu19.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1hu19.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1hvy1","title":"[SPORK][Registry][Core] GenServer Named Start Helper","description":"Provide helper that combines:
- acquire NameLease
- start GenServer under supervisor
- release lease on stop","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance:
- Behavior is cancel-correct and deterministic.
- Lab tests exist or are explicitly referenced.
- No stale names after stop/crash.","notes":"Implemented supervised named-start helper in src/gen_server.rs: added NamedGenServerStart + named_gen_server_start() (ChildStart adapter) that acquires NameLease via spawn_named_gen_server, stores GenServer task, and registers deterministic sync finalizer to cleanup_task(task_id) + resolve lease on region stop. Added regression tests named_start_helper_supervisor_stop_cleans_registry and named_start_helper_crash_then_stop_cleans_registry. Re-exported helper in src/spork.rs (spork::gen_server + prelude). Added SpawnError::NameRegistrationFailed in src/runtime/state.rs for deterministic name-collision surfacing in supervised start.\n\nValidation results:\n- cargo fmt --check: PASS (after cargo fmt)\n- cargo check --all-targets: initially PASS after implementation, later BLOCKED by unrelated shared-tree syntax error in src/cx/scope.rs (unexpected closing delimiter around line 1980).\n- cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings: BLOCKED by unrelated shared-tree breakages in src/combinator/circuit_breaker.rs and later src/cx/scope.rs.\n- cargo test: runs deep into suite, our unit tests pass, but full run fails on unrelated tests/distributed_spawn.rs and later shared-tree compile issues.\n","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentGlen","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:23:46.541881700Z","created_by":"FuchsiaRiver","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:57.343549231Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:43:57.343478690Z","close_reason":"Implemented named start helper + tests; local full gates currently blocked by unrelated shared-tree failures in other files","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["genserver","registry","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1hvy1","depends_on_id":"bd-133q8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:49Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1hvy1","depends_on_id":"bd-23az1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:49Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1i1l","title":"Actor mailbox: bounded channel with backpressure","description":"# Actor Mailbox Implementation

## Goal

Implement bounded mailboxes for actors that provide backpressure and integrate with the cancellation protocol.

## Background

Actors receive messages through mailboxes. Unlike unbounded queues (which can cause OOM), bounded mailboxes provide backpressure: when full, senders must wait or get backpressure signals.

## Design

### Mailbox<M>
- Wrapper around bounded mpsc channel
- Capacity specified at actor spawn time
- Integrates with two-phase send (reserve→commit)

### MailboxConfig
- capacity: usize (default: 64)
- overflow_policy: OverflowPolicy (Block, DropOldest, DropNewest, Error)
- priority_levels: Option<u8> (for priority mailboxes)

### MailboxHandle<M>
- Sender half given to ActorRef
- Methods: send(msg), try_send(msg), reserve() -> SendPermit

### MailboxReceiver<M>
- Receiver half held by ActorCell
- Methods: recv() -> Option<M>, try_recv(), is_empty(), len()

## Backpressure Semantics

When mailbox is full:
1. Block (default): Sender awaits until space available
2. DropOldest: Remove oldest message, insert new one
3. DropNewest: Reject new message immediately
4. Error: Return MailboxFull error

## Cancellation Integration

- recv() respects cancellation checkpoints
- Pending sends are aborted cleanly on actor cancellation
- SendPermit is an obligation (must commit or abort)

## Priority Mailbox (Optional)

For actors that need priority message handling:
- Multiple internal queues by priority level
- High-priority messages processed first
- Prevents priority inversion

## Implementation Notes

- Build on existing channel primitives in src/channel/
- MailboxConfig stored in ActorCell
- Metrics: messages_received, messages_dropped, current_depth

## Testing

- Backpressure: verify senders block when full
- Cancellation: verify clean abort of pending sends
- Priority: verify high-priority messages processed first
- Lab runtime: deterministic message ordering

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Mailbox<M> with configurable capacity
- [ ] OverflowPolicy enum with 4 strategies
- [ ] Two-phase send integration (SendPermit)
- [ ] Cancellation-safe recv()
- [ ] Optional priority levels
- [ ] Metrics for observability","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:17:53.139171701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T07:34:32.877353221Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T07:34:32.877175611Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["actors","mailbox","phase3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i1l","depends_on_id":"bd-3t9g","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i1l","depends_on_id":"bd-trgk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:34Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1i1w","title":"[ALIEN][E2E][GEO] End-to-end: canonical replay normalization + deterministic report","description":"## Goal

Provide an end-to-end harness that proves geodesic normalization is useful *in practice* and stays deterministic.

This is not a micro-unit test: it exercises the full surface area that users care about:
- trace capture -> poset build -> normalization (exact/heuristic) -> replay
- stable artifacts (goldens) + explainable decision ledger (when enabled)

## Scenario corpus

Create a small but high-leverage corpus of deterministic lab scenarios + captured traces that exhibit:
- many commutations (low structure)
- structured "two lanes" patterns
- cancellation / obligation-heavy traces
- known DPOR counterexamples where canonicalization makes the trace smaller/cleaner

Each scenario must have:
- fixed seed + fixed config
- an oracle: (a) quiescence, (b) no obligation leaks, (c) loser drain where relevant

## Report + logging requirements

- Core runtime code must not write to stdout/stderr.
- The harness should emit a deterministic JSON report (or trace-backed text report) that includes:
  - original trace length, normalized trace length
  - switch cost before/after
  - whether exact solver or heuristic path was used (with reason + bound)
  - optional galaxy-brain ledger excerpt (bounded size)

## Acceptance criteria

- Running the harness twice yields byte-identical reports (given same seed/config).
- For at least one scenario, switch cost strictly decreases vs baseline replay, without changing observable outcomes.
- Includes a golden-output mechanism (checksums) so regressions are caught automatically in CI.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:17:35.934422618Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T11:39:56.678078679Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T11:33:15.746573591Z","close_reason":"E2E harness created: tests/e2e_geodesic_normalization.rs with 5 scenarios (two_lane_simple, three_tasks_interleaved, many_tasks_convergence, single_task_baseline, four_tasks_high_contention), 6 acceptance tests (deterministic report, switch cost improvement, valid linear extensions, geodesic<=foata, golden checksum 0x80e9146892cb67fc, no oracle violations). All 14 tests pass, all scenarios show improvement.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","canonicalization","e2e","testing","trace"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i1w","depends_on_id":"bd-1scf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i1w","depends_on_id":"bd-2jwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3490","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i1w","depends_on_id":"bd-3dre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:32Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1204,"issue_id":"bd-1i1w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyReef: Added cancel-drain and obligation-interleave scenarios, updated golden checksum. 7 scenarios total, 14 tests pass. Commit fee4ea4.","created_at":"2026-02-03T11:39:56Z"}]}

@@ -176,6 +176,7 @@ toml = "0.8"

 [[bench]]
